node_types.description: Per-asset close-price history visible at the evaluation instant (availability-gated; assets without data keep an empty history).

doc.semantics: Availability-gated: only observations knowable at or before the evaluation instant are visible (no look-ahead). An asset with no visible observations keeps an empty history rather than being dropped; nothing is forward-filled.
doc.summary: The machine's data entry point — loads each universe asset's close-price history as it was known at the evaluation instant. Everything the strategy computes flows from here.

node_types.description: Per-asset left > right over the full bound domain; a missing operand yields false (never omission), with a missing-data trace event.

doc.semantics: Preserves the full bound domain: every asset appears in the output mask. If either operand is missing for an asset, its result is false (never omitted), with a missing-operand trace event.
doc.summary: Turns two cross-sections into a per-asset yes/no signal — asks 'is left greater than right?' for every asset (e.g. price above its moving average). This is the machine's condition/gate stage.

node_types.description: The graph terminal: receives the final PortfolioTargets. The engine — not the graph — reconciles targets into orders.

doc.semantics: The single graph terminal; consumes the final PortfolioTargets and emits no value. Turning targets into orders is engine territory (there is no order-generation node).
doc.summary: The machine's output — the final PortfolioTargets the strategy graph produces. The engine (not the graph) reconciles these targets into orders and fills.

node_types.description: Zeroes weights where the mask is false or missing (traced); survivors keep their sleeves — no renormalization, zeroed weight becomes cash.

doc.semantics: Zeroes weights where the mask is false OR missing (traced); survivors keep their sleeves — no renormalization, so the zeroed weight becomes cash.
doc.summary: Gates an allocation by a boolean signal — zeroes the weight of any asset the mask rejects. This is where a signal stage actually removes names from the book.

node_types.description: 1/|selected| per selected asset (renormalized across the selection); an empty selection is all cash.

doc.semantics: Renormalizes across the selected set. An empty selection yields all cash. Cash = 1 - Σw.
doc.summary: Weights the selected assets equally — the machine's simplest allocation stage, splitting the portfolio evenly across whatever was selected.

node_types.description: A fixed sleeve per universe asset (a number, or 'equal' for 1/|universe|); no renormalization — unallocated weight is cash.

doc.formula: w = weight_per_asset (or 1 / |universe| when 'equal') per universe asset

weight_per_asset.help: A fixed fraction per asset, or 'equal' for 1/|universe|.
weight_per_asset.label: Weight per asset

doc.semantics: Never renormalizes; unallocated weight is cash. A numeric weight whose total across the universe exceeds 1 fails loudly. An empty universe yields all cash.
doc.summary: Assigns each universe asset a fixed sleeve — the machine's allocation stage for a static, non-renormalized book (used with a downstream mask).

node_types.description: The n best-ranked universe assets (smallest score wins; ties by canonical ticker); unscored assets are excluded, and fewer than n may qualify.

n.help: How many top-ranked assets to hold (fewer if fewer qualify).
n.label: Number to select

doc.semantics: Consumes rank/score values (smallest score = best, designed for transform.rank output); ties resolve by ascending canonical ticker. Universe assets without a score are excluded (traced); if fewer than n qualify, all qualifying assets are selected.
doc.summary: Selects the n best-ranked assets from the universe — the machine's shortlist step that narrows a scored cross-section down to the names actually held.

node_types.description: Caps each weight at max and redistributes the excess via the deterministic proportional waterfall; an unresolvable remainder stays in cash.

doc.formula: w(asset) ≤ max;  excess redistributed proportionally to uncapped assets

max.help: Ceiling on any single asset's weight (0–1); excess is redistributed.
max.label: Max weight

doc.semantics: Deterministic waterfall: cap every over-limit asset at max, redistribute the excess proportionally across eligible uncapped assets, repeat until no asset exceeds the cap. If no eligible capacity remains, the remainder stays in cash — the cap is never violated to force full investment.
doc.summary: Caps concentration — limits any single asset's weight to max and redistributes the excess across the others. The machine's risk-control stage.

node_types.description: The value at the latest visible session, per asset; assets without an observation at that session are excluded.

doc.formula: value(asset) = series(asset) at the latest visible session
doc.semantics: Takes each asset's value AT the latest visible session only; an asset without an observation at that exact session is excluded (no stale substitution). Warm-up: 0 — needs only the current session.
doc.summary: Collapses a per-asset history into a single current value per asset — the explicit 'take the latest point' step (e.g. latest price or latest moving average) that feeds cross-sectional logic.

node_types.description: Simple moving average over the trailing window sessions; sessions missing any window observation get no point (never forward-filled).

window.help: Trailing sessions averaged into each moving-average point.
window.label: Window

doc.semantics: Simple average over the trailing window sessions ending at each session. A session missing any observation in its window produces no point (never forward-filled). Warm-up: window - 1 prior sessions.
doc.summary: Smooths each asset's price into a trailing moving average — the trend line a strategy compares the current price against.

node_types.description: Ordinal ranks over the present values (1 = best; descending by default); ties broken by ascending canonical ticker.

descending.help: When true (default), the highest score gets rank 1.
descending.label: Descending

doc.semantics: Ranks only the assets with a present score (excluded assets are not ranked); ties are broken by ascending canonical ticker, deterministically.
doc.summary: Orders the universe by a score — assigns ordinal ranks (1 = best) so a downstream selection stage can pick the top names. The machine's ranking step.

node_types.description: Return over the trailing lookback_sessions calendar sessions: close(D)/close(D-L) - 1. Assets missing either close are excluded.

lookback_sessions.help: Calendar sessions back to the anchor close (the momentum window).
lookback_sessions.label: Lookback sessions

doc.semantics: D is the latest visible session; D-L is exactly lookback_sessions calendar sessions earlier. An asset missing either close, or with a zero anchor close, is excluded (traced) — never forward-filled. Warm-up: lookback_sessions prior sessions.
doc.summary: Measures each asset's momentum as its return over the trailing lookback window — the raw signal this strategy ranks on.

node_types.description: A fixed list of asset tickers, emitted in canonical (ascending) order.

tickers.help: Asset symbols in the universe (e.g. SPY, QQQ); emitted ascending.
tickers.label: Tickers

doc.semantics: Emits the tickers in canonical (ascending) order. Reads no market data; the list is taken verbatim from the tickers parameter.
doc.summary: Defines the machine's investable universe — the fixed set of asset tickers every downstream stage is allowed to consider. This is where the strategy declares what it can trade.
